typecheck.command: go build -o test-output ./cmd/server; if (Test-Path '.\\test-output.exe') { Remove-Item -Force '.\\test-output.exe' }; if (Test-Path '.\\test-output') { Remove-Item -Force '.\\test-output' }

lint.command: $base = git merge-base HEAD origin/dev; $range = "$base...HEAD"; $files = git diff --name-only $range -- '*.go'; foreach ($f in $files) { gofmt -l $f }

blockingIssues.description: The import-removal fix was appended to unrelated PR #2864 / branch `claude-cache-fix/F-1.5` instead of being delivered as its own one-file PR scoped to `test/thinking_conversion_test.go`.

The registry-test repair itself is correct, but PR #2865 does not meet the mission's required CI-gate evidence: `gh pr checks` shows only the agents-md-guard result and not the required `pr-test-build` and `translator-path-guard` successes.

The recorded F-0.3 commit is an unrelated registry-test change; no formatting-only commit/PR was produced for `internal/config/config.go`, `internal/config/config_cloak_levers_test.go`, and `internal/watcher/diff/config_diff.go`.
The rewritten strict-mode cloaking test no longer verifies that strict mode suppresses forwarding the original system prompt into the first user message, so the intended regression remains uncovered.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Split Milestone D away from the stale 'Claude Cache Fixes' PR-chain procedure, or provide a Milestone D-specific worker skill that matches the actual feature set and required workflow.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: F-0.1, F-0.2, F-0.3, and F-4.1 reviewers all found workers bypassing the assigned `claude-cache-pr-worker` flow because the mission AGENTS and skill content still described the earlier cache-fix PR chain rather than Milestone D repairs.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Either populate repo-root `.factory/services.yaml` and `.factory/library/*` for this workspace or stop directing workers/reviewers to those paths when they are absent.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Multiple reviews plus this validator session hit ENOENT for `G:\Droid.Claude\CLIProxyAPI\router-dev\.factory\services.yaml` and `.factory\library\...`; `LS .factory` shows only `validation/`, so the advertised canonical command and knowledge files are unavailable.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Align the contract's required PR checks with the automation that actually runs on Milestone D PRs, or restore the missing checks before using them as scrutiny blockers.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: F-0.2 review found that `gh pr checks 2865` exposed only the agents-md-guard result, while the contract still requires `pr-test-build`, `agents-md-guard`, and `translator-path-guard` successes for every PR.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Do not assign PR-only skills to local workspace cleanup features, and allow successful no-commit handoffs for explicit local-only fixes.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: F-0.4 is a local cleanup that explicitly must not create a PR or commit, yet it was assigned `claude-cache-pr-worker` and the worker had to attach ambient HEAD because success handoffs currently require a `commitId`.

rejectedObservations.reason: duplicate — kept once as a systemic recommendation and rejected repeated copies from multiple reviews.

rejectedObservations.observation: Workers bypassed the assigned Milestone D skill flow
rejectedObservations.reason: duplicate — captured once as a mission/skill guidance recommendation rather than repeating near-identical reports from several features.

rejectedObservations.observation: Isolation rules for one-file fixes should not be bypassed
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented — current mission instructions already require workers to invoke assigned skills and keep feature scope isolated; the failure was execution drift, not missing written guidance.
